text: STEMSight PIM (Parkinson's Involuntary Movements Detection): A real-time medical AI system that detects 10 classes of Parkinson's involuntary movements using UNIK (Graph Convolutional Networks). Built with Python, PyTorch, FastAPI, and MediaPipe. Achieved 82.88% accuracy on 2600 samples. Uses a spatial-temporal GCN model trained on skeleton data extracted from video.

For the PIM project, I built the full data pipeline: skeleton extraction using MediaPipe PoseLandmarker, 80/20 train/test split, and training the UNIK GCN model. The model processes 300 frames at 60 FPS per sample and classifies movements including tremor, dystonia, chorea, myoclonus, and others.

AI Combination Server: A scalable multi-feature AI backend platform built with FastAPI. Supports chat, summarization, and suggestion features across multiple contexts. Uses retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with sentence-transformers for semantic retrieval, a cross-encoder for reranking, and configurable external LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini). Includes SSE streaming for real-time chat responses.

NeuroSpring Hospital Camera Detection Platform: A real-time patient-monitoring system built for hospitals to detect involuntary movement patterns and enable rapid ambulance deployment. Built cross-platform UIs using React and TypeScript for web and Raspberry Pi devices, deployed backend APIs with FastAPI, managed data using Supabase for real-time processing, and integrated MediaPipe with the UNIK AI model for posture and movement analysis.

FanFly iOS App: Led development of the company's first iOS application connecting fans and artists for show ticket sales. Built with modular SwiftUI components and reusable UI templates that reduced feature development time by 30%. Resolved memory issues that boosted app usage by 40%. Authored onboarding documentation for the engineering team.

Naturalization Study App: Developed and launched an iOS app for the U.S. naturalization test featuring adaptive flashcards, quizzes, and analytics. Added multi-language support, used Firebase Remote Config for real-time content updates, and implemented offline study with sync capabilities. Published on the Apple App Store.
Portfolio Website: A responsive personal portfolio website built with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS. Hosted on AWS Amplify with automated CI/CD pipeline for continuous deployment.

Scoop Carpooling App: Contributed to the iOS app for a carpooling and commuting platform. Converted Figma designs into responsive SwiftUI and UIKit interfaces, and integrated REST APIs using PromiseKit and Alamofire for smooth asynchronous data interactions.
